question: Show me an example of adapters overview

sources.title: Adapters Overview
sources.content:
| Adapter | Environment | Persistence |
|---------|-------------|-------------|
| MemoryAdapter | Node, browser | Not persisted |
| IndexedDBAdapter | Browser only | Persists in IndexedDB |

sources.title: Adapters Overview
sources.content:
All adapters implement transaction(fn). If the callback throws, all changes
are rolled back:

sources.title: Adapters Overview
sources.content:
Any object implementing the StorageAdapter interface works:

Full StorageAdapter interface in API reference.

sources.title: Adapters Overview
sources.content: Adapters handle persistence. ctrodb ships with two.

sources.title: Plugin Overview
sources.content:
For a create() call:
onBeforeCreate — plugins run in order, each can modify data
Schema defaults applied
Schema validation
Adapter creates record
onAfterCreate — plugins run in order

For update():
onBeforeUpdate — plugins can modify changes
Schema validation (merged with existing)
Adapter updates record
onAfterUpdate

For delete():
onBeforeDelete
Adapter deletes record
onAfterDelete

sources.title: MemoryAdapter
sources.description: In-memory storage for Node and testing
sources.content:
Each collection is a Map
IDs are auto-incrementing numbers (1, 2, 3, ...)
On schema connect, collections are pre-created
Transactions take a snapshot and restore on error

sources.title: Plugin Overview
sources.content:
For onBeforeCreate and onBeforeUpdate, the return value replaces the data
if not undefined. This lets plugins transform data before it is stored.
